devanagari: देवर्षिनारदकर्तृकं भगवतो गृहचर्यादर्शनम् । श्रीशुक उवाच । नरकं निहतं श्रुत्वा तथोद्वाहं च योषिताम् । कृष्णेनैकेन बह्वीनां तद् दिदृक्षुः...
english_devnagari: devarṣināradakartṛkaṃ bhagavato gṛhacaryādarśanam śrīśuka uvāca narakaṃ nihataṃ śrutvā tathodvāhaṃ ca yoṣitām kṛṣṇenaikena bahvīnāṃ tad didṛkṣuḥ sma nāradaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Having heard that Narakāsura was killed and that Śrī Kṛṣṇa, a single individual as he was, married those numerous women, Nārada became eager to see how Kṛṣṇa managed to live with them all.

devanagari: चित्रं बतैतदेकेन वपुषा युगपत् पृथक् । गृहेषु द्व्यष्टसाहस्रं स्त्रिय एक उदावहत् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: citraṃ bataitadekena vapuṣā yugapat pṛthak gṛheṣu dvyaṣṭasāhasraṃ striya eka udāvahat
translation: It is really miraculous that one person (Śrī Kṛṣṇa) possessing a single personality, could severally and simultaneously marry sixteen thousand women, each in a separate house.

devanagari: इत्युत्सुको द्वारवतीं देवर्षिर्द्रष्टुमागमत् । पुष्पितोपवनाराम द्विजालिकुलनादिताम् ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: ityutsuko dvāravatīṃ devarṣirdraṣṭumāgamat puṣpitopavanārāma dvijālikulanāditām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: Being curious to know this, the divine sage Nārada came to see this to Dvārakā which appeared beautiful with its parks and gardens in full blossom and was resonant with the notes of singing birds and humming of the swarms of bees.

devanagari: उत्फुल्लेन्दीवराम्भोज कह्लारकुमुदोत्पलैः । छुरितेषु सरःसूच्चैः कूजितां हंससारसैः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: utphullendīvarāmbhoja kahlārakumudotpalaiḥ churiteṣu saraḥsūccaiḥ kūjitāṃ haṃsasārasaiḥ
translation: It was resounded with the sweet warblings and melodious notes of swans and other waterbirds which swarmed the surrounding ponds and pools covered with full blown lotuses, lilies, Kalhāras and lotuses that bloom by day and by night.

devanagari: प्रासादलक्षैर्नवभिः जुष्टां स्फाटिकराजतैः । महामरकतप्रख्यैः स्वर्णरत्‍नपरिच्छदैः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: prāsādalakṣairnavabhiḥ juṣṭāṃ sphāṭikarājataiḥ mahāmarakataprakhyaiḥ svarṇarat‍naparicchadaiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ juṣ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ juṣ class 3 verb], [accusative single from √ juṣ class 6 verb]

translation: (Nārada came to Dvārakā) which was thickly studded with nine hundred thousands of palatial buildings constructed with crystals and silver and was shining with excellent emeralds thickly set therein and furnished with articles (of furniture) plated with gold and adorned with jewels.

devanagari: विभक्तरथ्यापथचत्वरापणैः । शालासभाभी रुचिरां सुरालयैः । संसिक्तमार्गाङ्गनवीथिदेहलीं । पतत्पताका ध्वजवारितातपाम् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: vibhaktarathyāpathacatvarāpaṇaiḥ śālāsabhābhī rucirāṃ surālayaiḥ saṃsiktamārgāṅganavīthidehalīṃ patatpatākā dhvajavāritātapām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ pat class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ pat class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ pat class 1 verb]
[imperfect active first single], [aorist active first single]

translation: The city looked beautiful with its streets, roads, quadrangles and market places in the city distinctly laid out; with stalls, assembly-halls and shrines dedicated to gods. The roads, court-yards, foot-paths and threshholds of the city were profusely sprinkled with water. The heat of the sun in that city was warded off by everflying flags and pennants.

devanagari: तस्यामन्तःपुरं श्रीमद् अर्चितं सर्वधिष्ण्यपैः । हरेः स्वकौशलं यत्र त्वष्ट्रा कार्त्स्न्येन दर्शितम् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: tasyāmantaḥpuraṃ śrīmad arcitaṃ sarvadhiṣṇyapaiḥ hareḥ svakauśalaṃ yatra tvaṣṭrā kārtsnyena darśitam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ ṛc ], [accusative single from √ ṛc ]
[nominative single from √ ṛc ], [accusative single from √ ṛc ]
[nominative single from √ dṛś ], [accusative single from √ dṛś ]

translation: In that city, there was (a complex of palatial buildings known as) the harem of Lord Kṛṣṇa which was highly praised by all the presiding deities protecting different spheres (in the universe). In constructing that (palatial building complex), Tvaṣṭṛ, the celestial architect had exhibited all his architectural skill and ingenuity.

devanagari: तत्र षोडशभिः सद्म सहस्रैः समलङ्कृतम् । विवेशैकतमं शौरेः पत्‍नीनां भवनं महत् ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: tatra ṣoḍaśabhiḥ sadma sahasraiḥ samalaṅkṛtam viveśaikatamaṃ śaureḥ pat‍nīnāṃ bhavanaṃ mahat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: That part of the city was adorned with sixteen thousand palatial buildings of Kṛṣṇa’s queens. Nārada entered into one of the big palaces out of them.

devanagari: विष्टब्धं विद्रुमस्तंभैः वैदूर्यफलकोत्तमैः । इन्द्रनीलमयैः कुड्यैः जगत्या चाहतत्विषा ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: viṣṭabdhaṃ vidrumastaṃbhaiḥ vaidūryaphalakottamaiḥ indranīlamayaiḥ kuḍyaiḥ jagatyā cāhatatviṣā
translation: It was supported on columns of coral and the ceiling was of Vaidūrya (a precious stone). Its walls were of sapphire and its floor of unfading lustre was paved with the same precious stones.

devanagari: वितानैर्निर्मितैस्त्वष्ट्रा मुक्तादामविलम्बिभिः । दान्तैरासनपर्यङ्कैः मण्युत्तमपरिष्कृतैः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: vitānairnirmitaistvaṣṭrā muktādāmavilambibhiḥ dāntairāsanaparyaṅkaiḥ maṇyuttamapariṣkṛtaiḥ
translation: It was also furnished with canopies specially designed by Tvaṣṭṛ from which wreaths of pearls were hanging and it (was provided) with seats and beds made of ivory and set with excellent jewels.

devanagari: दासीभिर्निष्ककण्ठीभिः सुवासोभिरलङ्कृतम् । पुम्भिः सकञ्चुकोष्णीष सुवस्त्रमणिकुण्डलैः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: dāsībhirniṣkakaṇṭhībhiḥ suvāsobhiralaṅkṛtam pumbhiḥ sakañcukoṣṇīṣa suvastramaṇikuṇḍalaiḥ
translation: It was staffed with maid-servants adorned with necklaces of gold-coins and wearing costly apparel and with men-servants wearing coats, turbans, excellent dress and diamond-ear-rings.

devanagari: रत्‍नप्रदीपनिकरद्युतिभिर्निरस्त । ध्वान्तं विचित्रवलभीषु शिखण्डिनोऽङ्ग । नृत्यन्ति यत्र विहितागुरुधूपमक्षैः । निर्यान्तमीक्ष्य घनबुद्ध...
english_devnagari: rat‍napradīpanikaradyutibhirnirasta dhvāntaṃ vicitravalabhīṣu śikhaṇḍino nṛtyanti yatra vihitāgurudhūpamakṣaiḥ niryāntamīkṣya ghanabuddhaya unnadantaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ nṛt class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ nṛt class 4 verb], [accusative plural from √ nṛt class 4 verb]

translation: O child Parīkṣit. Darkness in that place was dispelled by the lustre of clusters of jewels radiating light like lamps. In them, peacocks perching in picturesque (artificial) caves screamed loudly and danced gaily regarding (foolishly) the clouds of fumes of aloewood issuing out of the windows, to be the real clouds.

devanagari: तस्मिन्समानगुणरूपवयःसुवेष । दासीसहस्रयुतयानुसवं गृहिण्या । विप्रो ददर्श चमरव्यजनेन रुक्म । दण्डेन सात्वतपतिं परिवीजयन्त्या ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: tasminsamānaguṇarūpavayaḥsuveṣa dāsīsahasrayutayānusavaṃ gṛhiṇyā vipro dadarśa camaravyajanena rukma daṇḍena sātvatapatiṃ parivījayantyā

translation: The Brāhmaṇa sage Nārada saw there Kṛṣṇa, the Lord of Sātvatas who was being fanned with a chowrie provided with a gold handle by his consort who in her turn was waited upon constantly by a thousand maid-servants who were all equal (to each other) in nature, beauty, age and excellent dress.

devanagari: तं सन्निरीक्ष्य भगवान् सहसोत्थितश्री । पर्यङ्कतः सकलधर्मभृतां वरिष्ठः । आनम्य पादयुगलं शिरसा किरीट । जुष्टेन साञ्जलिरवीविशदासने स्वे ॥...
english_devnagari: taṃ sannirīkṣya bhagavān sahasotthitaśrī paryaṅkataḥ sakaladharmabhṛtāṃ variṣṭhaḥ ānamya pādayugalaṃ śirasā kirīṭa juṣṭena sāñjaliravīviśadāsane sve

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [instrumental single from √ juṣ class 1 verb], [instrumental single from √ juṣ class 3 verb], [instrumental single from √ juṣ class 6 verb]
[instrumental single from √ juṣ class 1 verb], [instrumental single from √ juṣ class 3 verb], [instrumental single from √ juṣ class 6 verb]

translation: On seeing him, the glorious Lord, the foremost among those who observe the injunctions of Dharma, promptly got up from the bed of Rukmiṇī (the goddess Śrī), paid respects to his feet with his head adorned with a diadem. With folded palms, He made Nārada occupy his own (exalted) seat.

devanagari: तस्यावनिज्य चरणौ तदपः स्वमूर्ध्ना । बिभ्रज्जगद्गुरुतमोऽपि सतां पतिर्हि । ब्रह्मण्यदेव इति यद्गुणनाम युक्तं । तस्यैव यच्चरणशौचमशेषतीर्थ...
english_devnagari: tasyāvanijya caraṇau tadapaḥ svamūrdhnā bibhrajjagadgurutamo brahmaṇyadeva iti yadguṇanāma yuktaṃ tasyaiva yaccaraṇaśaucamaśeṣatīrtham

translation: Though he himself was the Creator and Preceptor of the universe and the chief among the righteous, he washed Nārada’s feet and bore that water on his own head. The epithet Brahmaṇya-deva (God who is friendly to Brāhmaṇas) is most appropriately applicable to him both in its implication and as a title. For the waters that wash his feet (viz. the river Gaṅgā) sanctifies the rest of the world.

devanagari: संपूज्य देवऋषिवर्यमृषिः पुराणो । नारायणो नरसखो विधिनोदितेन । वाण्याभिभाष्य मितयामृतमिष्टया तं । प्राह प्रभो भगवते करवाम हे किम् ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: saṃpūjya devaṛṣivaryamṛṣiḥ purāṇo nārāyaṇo narasakho vidhinoditena vāṇyābhibhāṣya mitayāmṛtamiṣṭayā taṃ prāha prabho bhagavate karavāma he kim

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vṛ ], [accusative single from √ vṛ ]
[instrumental single from √ mā class 2 verb], [instrumental single from √ mā class 3 verb], [instrumental single from √ mā class 4 verb]

translation: Having worshipped the prominent divine sage (Nārada), the Ancient sage Nārāyaṇa, the friend and companion of the sage Nara, addressed him in measured words sweet like nectar and requested “O Lord Nārada! what service should we render to you?”

devanagari: श्रीनारद उवाच । नैवाद्भुतं त्वयि विभोऽखिललोकनाथे । मैत्री जनेषु सकलेषु दमः खलानाम् । निःश्रेयसाय हि जगत्स्थितिरक्षणाभ्यां । स्वैरावतार...
english_devnagari: śrīnārada uvāca naivādbhutaṃ tvayi vibho maitrī janeṣu sakaleṣu damaḥ khalānām niḥśreyasāya hi jagatsthitirakṣaṇābhyāṃ svairāvatāra urugāya vidāma suṣṭhu

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Nārada said:
“O almighty Lord! It is not at all wonderful in you who are the Protector of all the worlds that you entertain friendliness to all people as well as chastise the wicked (there is no contradiction in both these roles). You incarnate yourself at your own sweet will for conferring final Beatitude as well as for the maintenance and protection of the world. We realize this quite well, O Lord who are extolled as the highest one in the Vedas.

devanagari: दृष्टं तवाङ्घ्रियुगलं जनतापवर्गं । ब्रह्मादिभिर्हृदि विचिन्त्यमगाधबोधैः । संसारकूपपतितोत्तरणावलम्बं । ध्यायंश्चराम्यनुगृहाण यथा स्मृति...
english_devnagari: dṛṣṭaṃ tavāṅghriyugalaṃ janatāpavargaṃ brahmādibhirhṛdi vicintyamagādhabodhaiḥ saṃsārakūpapatitottaraṇāvalambaṃ dhyāyaṃścarāmyanugṛhāṇa yathā smṛtiḥ syāt

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ dṛś class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ dṛś class 1 verb]
[instrumental single from √ jan class 1 verb], [instrumental single from √ jan class 2 verb]
[instrumental single from √ jan class 1 verb], [instrumental single from √ jan class 2 verb]

translation: I have the good fortune of being blessed with the sight of the pair of your feet which in themselves are the blessed state of final Emancipation and which deserve to be meditated upon in their hearts by gods of unfathomable spiritual wisdom like Brahmā and others and which form the support to those who have fallen in the (dark) well of saṃsāra as a means to get out of it. (Hence I have accomplished my purpose). You, however, be pleased to favour me with unfailing, constant memory of your feet contemplating on which I shall move about in the world.”

devanagari: ततोऽन्यदाविशद् गेहं कृष्णपत्‍न्याः स नारदः । योगेश्वरेश्वरस्याङ्ग योगमायाविवित्सया ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: tato yogeśvareśvarasyāṅga yogamāyāvivitsayā
translation: Thereupon, in order to fathom and know the Yogic power of the Lord of the masters of Yoga, that sage Nārada entered the mansion of another queen of Śrī Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: दीव्यन्तमक्षैस्तत्रापि प्रियया चोद्धवेन च । पूजितः परया भक्त्या प्रत्युत्थानासनादिभिः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: dīvyantamakṣaistatrāpi priyayā coddhavena ca pūjitaḥ parayā bhaktyā pratyutthānāsanādibhiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ pūj class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ pūj class 10 verb]

translation: Even there he found Kṛṣṇa playing dice with his beloved consort Satyabhāmā and Uddhava. He was worshipped with utmost devotion by Kṛṣṇa by rising up to receive him, offering a comfortable seat and such other formalities.

devanagari: पृष्टश्चाविदुषेवासौ कदाऽयातो भवानिति । क्रियते किं नु पूर्णानां अपूर्णैरस्मदादिभिः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: pṛṣṭaścāviduṣevāsau kadā kriyate kiṃ nu pūrṇānāṃ apūrṇairasmadādibhiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle third single], [present passive third single]
[genitive plural from √ pṝ class 3 verb], [genitive plural from √ pṝ class 6 verb], [genitive plural from √ pṝ class 9 verb]
[genitive plural from √ pṝ class 3 verb], [genitive plural from √ pṝ class 6 verb], [genitive plural from √ pṝ class 9 verb]
[genitive plural from √ pṝ class 3 verb], [genitive plural from √ pṝ class 6 verb], [genitive plural from √ pṝ class 9 verb]

translation: Nārada was enquired of by Kṛṣṇa showing as if he was not cognisant of Nārada’s arrival. He asked, “When did you come, O worshipful sage? We are after all imperfect beings. What service may we render to perfect (or persons established in the Self) sages like you?

devanagari: अथापि ब्रूहि नो ब्रह्मन् जन्मैतच्छोभनं कुरु । स तु विस्मित उत्थाय तूष्णीमन्यदगाद् गृहम् ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: athāpi brūhi no brahman janmaitacchobhanaṃ kuru sa tu vismita utthāya tūṣṇīmanyadagād gṛham
translation: O Brāhmaṇa sage! Be pleased to order us and make this life of ours blessed (by giving us an opportunity to serve you)”. Nārada was extremely surprised. He rose up without a word and went co another house (of another queen of Kṛṣṇa).

devanagari: तत्राप्यचष्ट गोविन्दं लालयन्तं सुतान् शिशून् । ततोऽन्यस्मिन्गृहेऽपश्यन् मज्जनाय कृतोद्यमम् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: tatrāpyacaṣṭa govindaṃ lālayantaṃ sutān śiśūn tato

translation: Even there, he saw Lord Kṛṣṇa playing and fondling with his infant sons. Then in another house (of another queen), he saw him preparing for his bath.

devanagari: जुह्वन्तं च वितानाग्नीन् यजन्तं पञ्चभिर्मखैः । भोजयन्तं द्विजान् क्वापि भुञ्जानमवशेषितम् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: juhvantaṃ ca vitānāgnīn yajantaṃ pañcabhirmakhaiḥ bhojayantaṃ dvijān kvāpi bhuñjānamavaśeṣitam

[nominative single from √ iṣ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ iṣ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ iṣ class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ iṣ class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ iṣ ], [accusative single from √ iṣ ]

translation: At some houses, Nārada saw him offering oblations to sacrificial fire, at other places, he was seen worshipping God by performing five kinds of daily sacrifices (called mahāyajñas); at some houses, he saw him feeding the Brāhmaṇas with a feast and at another place, he found him taking food after offering oblations to the sacred fire.

devanagari: क्वापि सन्ध्यामुपासीनं जपन्तं ब्रह्म वाग्यतम् । एकत्र चासिचर्माभ्यां चरन्तमसिवर्त्मसु ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: kvāpi sandhyāmupāsīnaṃ japantaṃ brahma vāgyatam ekatra cāsicarmābhyāṃ carantamasivartmasu
translation: At some houses, he was performing sandhyā (twilight prayer); at other places, he was contemplating the Gāyatrī observing complete silence. At some places, he was practising various skills in fencing with a sword and a shield.

devanagari: अश्वैर्गजै रथैः क्वापि विचरन्तं गदाग्रजम् । क्वचिच्छयानं पर्यङ्के स्तूयमानं च वन्दिभिः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: aśvairgajai rathaiḥ kvāpi vicarantaṃ gadāgrajam kvacicchayānaṃ paryaṅke stūyamānaṃ ca vandibhiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ śī class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ śī class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ stu class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ stu class 2 verb]

translation: At some other houses, he found Kṛṣṇa the elder brother of Gada riding horses, elephants or chariots. At some other mansions, he found him lying in bed while being extolled by bards (bandins).

devanagari: मंत्रयन्तं च कस्मिंश्चित् मंत्रिभिश्चोद्धवादिभिः । जलक्रीडारतं क्वापि वारमुख्याबलावृतम् ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: maṃtrayantaṃ ca kasmiṃścit maṃtribhiścoddhavādibhiḥ jalakrīḍārataṃ kvāpi vāramukhyābalāvṛtam
translation: In a certain house, he was holding consultations with his counsellors like Uddhava and others; while at other places, surrounded by a bevy of courtesans, he was indulging in water-sports.

devanagari: कुत्रचिद्द्विजमुख्येभ्यो ददतं गाः स्वलङ्कृताः । इतिहासपुराणानि श्रृण्वन्तं मङ्गलानि च ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: kutraciddvijamukhyebhyo dadataṃ gāḥ svalaṅkṛtāḥ itihāsapurāṇāni śrṛṇvantaṃ maṅgalāni ca
translation: At some houses, he found him giving highly adorned cows to prominent Brāhmaṇas, while at others he saw him listening to the auspicious stories from Itihāsa and Purāṇas.

devanagari: हसन्तं हासकथया कदाचित् प्रियया गृहे । क्वापि धर्मं सेवमानं अर्थकामौ च कुत्रचित् ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: hasantaṃ hāsakathayā kadācit priyayā gṛhe kvāpi dharmaṃ sevamānaṃ arthakāmau ca kutracit

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ sev class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ sev class 1 verb]

translation: In a certain house, he was laughing by telling a joke to his beloved; in other places, he was carefully attending to Dharma, while at other places he was looking after matters pertaining to finance and at other places he was indulging in personal enjoyment.

devanagari: ध्यायन्तमेकमासीनं पुरुषं प्रकृतेः परम् । शुश्रूषन्तं गुरून् क्वापि कामैर्भोगैः सपर्यया ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: dhyāyantamekamāsīnaṃ puruṣaṃ prakṛteḥ param śuśrūṣantaṃ gurūn kvāpi kāmairbhogaiḥ saparyayā
translation: At some houses he saw him sitting alone in meditation contemplating on the Supreme Lord transcendental to Prakṛti; at other places, he found him waiting upon the elders and the preceptors by offering them objects of enjoyment and articles of worship.

devanagari: कुर्वन्तं विग्रहं कैश्चित् सन्धिं चान्यत्र केशवम् । कुत्रापि सह रामेण चिन्तयन्तं सतां शिवम् ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: kurvantaṃ vigrahaṃ kaiścit sandhiṃ cānyatra keśavam kutrāpi saha rāmeṇa cintayantaṃ satāṃ śivam
translation: At some places, he was planning war against some kings, while at other places, he was negotiating peace; at some places he was conferring with Balarāma about the wellbeing of the saintly people.

devanagari: पुत्राणां दुहितॄणां च काले विध्युपयापनम् । दारैर्वरैस्तत्सदृशैः कल्पयन्तं विभूतिभिः ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: putrāṇāṃ duhitṝṇāṃ ca kāle vidhyupayāpanam dārairvaraistatsadṛśaiḥ kalpayantaṃ vibhūtibhiḥ
translation: At some place, he saw him celebrating the marriages of his sons and daughters at the proper age with brides and bridegrooms suitable to their status and royal fortune.

devanagari: प्रस्थापनोपनयनैः अपत्यानां महोत्सवान् । वीक्ष्य योगेश्वरेशस्य येषां लोका विसिस्मिरे ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: prasthāpanopanayanaiḥ apatyānāṃ mahotsavān vīkṣya yogeśvareśasya yeṣāṃ lokā visismire
translation: People were wonḍerstruck to see the enthusiasm of that Lord of Yoga in conducting festivities at the time of giving send off to children or welcoming those who returned (for example daughters going to their husbands’ houses or returning therefrom after a long stay).

devanagari: यजन्तं सकलान् देवान् क्वापि क्रतुभिरूर्जितैः । पूर्तयन्तं क्वचिद् धर्मं कूर्पाराममठादिभिः ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: yajantaṃ sakalān devān kvāpi kratubhirūrjitaiḥ pūrtayantaṃ kvacid dharmaṃ kūrpārāmamaṭhādibhiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [instrumental plural from √ ūrj class 1 verb], [instrumental plural from √ ūrj class 2 verb], [instrumental plural from √ ūrj class 7 verb]

[vocative single from √ pṝ class 3 verb], [vocative single from √ pṝ class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ pṝ class 9 verb]

translation: Sometimes he saw Kṛṣṇa worshipping all gods with elaborate sacrifices. Sometimes he was seen engaged in performing the pūrta duties of religion by digging wells, founding groves, constructing temples, etc., for public weal.

devanagari: चरन्तं मृगयां क्वापि हयमारुह्य सैन्धवम् । घ्नन्तं तत्र पशून् मेध्यान् परीतं यदुपुङ्गवैः ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: carantaṃ mṛgayāṃ kvāpi hayamāruhya saindhavam ghnantaṃ tatra paśūn medhyān parītaṃ yadupuṅgavaiḥ
translation: At some places, he saw Kṛṣṇa riding a pedigree horse from the Sindhu country and chasing a game. Sometimes followed by Yādava warriors, he hunted animals useful for sacrifice.

devanagari: अव्यक्तलिङ्गं प्रकृतिषु अन्तःपुरगृहादिषु । क्वचिच्चरन्तं योगेशं तत्तद्भावबुभुत्सया ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: avyaktaliṅgaṃ prakṛtiṣu antaḥpuragṛhādiṣu kvaciccarantaṃ yogeśaṃ tattadbhāvabubhutsayā
translation: Somewhere he found him moving about incognito among his subjects, their houses, harems etc. with a view to understand the real feelings and plans of the people.

devanagari: अथोवाच हृषीकेशं नारदः प्रहसन्निव । योगमायोदयं वीक्ष्य मानुषीं ईयुषो गतिम् ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: athovāca hṛṣīkeśaṃ nāradaḥ prahasanniva yogamāyodayaṃ vīkṣya mānuṣīṃ īyuṣo gatim
translation: In this way, after observing this exhibition of Kṛṣṇa’s vast Yogic powers, though he was following the human way of life, Nārada addressed him with a smile.

devanagari: विदाम योगमायास्ते दुर्दर्शा अपि मायिनाम् । योगेश्वरात्मन् निर्भाता भवत्पादनिषेवया ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: vidāma yogamāyāste durdarśā api māyinām yogeśvarātman nirbhātā bhavatpādaniṣevayā
translation: “O Lord of Yoga! Your Māyā is incomprehensible—nay not even easily cognisable—to the masters of Māyās. But through the service of Your feet, our hearts are filled with the spiritual light. And we can comprehend your Yoga Māyā.

devanagari: अनुजानीहि मां देव लोकांस्ते यशसाऽप्लुतान् । पर्यटामि तवोद्गायन् लीला भुवनपावनीम् ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: anujānīhi māṃ deva lokāṃste yaśasā paryaṭāmi tavodgāyan līlā bhuvanapāvanīm
translation: O Lord Kṛṣṇa! Be pleased to give me leave. I shall range all over the worlds overflowing with your glory, celebrating in songs your sportive acts which sanctify the world.”

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । ब्रह्मन्धन्नस्य वक्ताहं कर्ता तदनुमोदिता । तच्छिक्षयन्लोकमिमं आस्थितः पुत्र मा खिदः ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvāca brahmandhannasya vaktāhaṃ kartā tadanumoditā tacchikṣayanlokamimaṃ āsthitaḥ putra mā khidaḥ

translation:
The Lord replied:
“Oh Brāhmaṇa sage. I am the promulgator of Dharma. It is I who practice it and grant my approbation to it. I have descended on this world for teaching it by my example. You need not get troubled, O child.”

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । इत्याचरन्तं सद्धर्मान् पावनान् गृहमेधिनाम् । तमेव सर्वगेहेषु सन्तमेकं ददर्श ह ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca ityācarantaṃ saddharmān pāvanān gṛhamedhinām tameva sarvageheṣu santamekaṃ dadarśa ha

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ sam class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ sam class 1 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
It is reported that Nārada saw one and the same Kṛṣṇa (present in all the houses of Kṛṣṇa’s queens) performing the righteous duties of householders which purify them.

devanagari: कृष्णस्यानन्तवीर्यस्य योगमायामहोदयम् । मुहुर्दृष्ट्वा ऋषिरभूद् विस्मितो जातकौतुकः ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: kṛṣṇasyānantavīryasya yogamāyāmahodayam muhurdṛṣṭvā ṛṣirabhūd vismito jātakautukaḥ
translation: Having observed time and anon the vast display of the Yogic powers of Kṛṣṇa of infinite prowess, the sage who became formerly curious about it, was overwhelmed with great astonishment.

devanagari: इत्यर्थकामधर्मेषु कृष्णेन श्रद्धितात्मना । सम्यक् सभाजितः प्रीतः तमेवानुस्मरन् ययौ ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: ityarthakāmadharmeṣu kṛṣṇena śraddhitātmanā samyak sabhājitaḥ prītaḥ tamevānusmaran yayau

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ prī class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ prī class 9 verb]
[nominative single from √ smṛ class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ smṛ class 1 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Nārada who was duly worshipped by Śrī Kṛṣṇa who showed His faith in dharma, aṛtha and kāma, the three objects of human life, was greatly pleased with him. And constantly contemplating upon him only, he went his way.

devanagari: एवं मनुष्यपदवीमनुवर्तमानो । नारायणोऽखिलभवाय गृहीतशक्तिः । रेमेऽङ्ग षोडशसहस्रवराङ्गनानां । सव्रीडसौहृदनिरीक्षणहासजुष्टः ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ manuṣyapadavīmanuvartamāno nārāyaṇo reme savrīḍasauhṛdanirīkṣaṇahāsajuṣṭaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[nominative single from √ juṣ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ juṣ class 3 verb], [nominative single from √ juṣ class 6 verb]

translation: O King! Following in this way the course of conduct as a human being, Lord Kṛṣṇa assumed different forms for the prosperity of the whole of the world; worshipped by the sixteen thousand beautiful damsels with their bashful, affectionate looks and smile, he spent his life delightfully (at Dvārakā).

devanagari: यानीह विश्वविलयोद्भववृत्तिहेतुः । कर्माण्यनन्यविषयाणि हरीश्चकार । यस्त्वङ्ग गायति श्रृणोत्यनुमोदते वा । भक्तिर्भवेद्भगवति ह्यपवर्गमार्...
english_devnagari: yānīha viśvavilayodbhavavṛttihetuḥ karmāṇyananyaviṣayāṇi harīścakāra yastvaṅga gāyati śrṛṇotyanumodate vā bhaktirbhavedbhagavati hyapavargamārge

translation: He who celebrates in songs, listens to or expresses approbation for the exploits of Hari which it is impossible for others to perform but which Hari, who is the cause of creation, maintenance and destruction of the universe, has done, will get engendered in him the devotion to the Lord Almighty which will lead to the Final Beatitude.

devanagari: इति श्रीमद्‍भागवते महापुराणे पारमहंस्यां । संहितायां दशमस्कन्धे उत्तरार्धे । कृष्णगार्हस्थ्यदर्शनं नाम एकोनसप्ततिमोऽध्यायः ॥ ६९ ॥
english_devnagari: iti śrīmad‍bhāgavate mahāpurāṇe pāramahaṃsyāṃ saṃhitāyāṃ daśamaskandhe uttarārdhe kṛṣṇagārhasthyadarśanaṃ nāma ekonasaptatimo
